title: Configure S3 Remote Backend
description: Write a Terraform configuration with an S3 backend for remote state storage with DynamoDB locking, and a remote state data source.
starterCode:
# Write a Terraform configuration that:
# 1. Configures an S3 backend with:
#    - bucket: "my-company-terraform-state"
#    - key: "production/app/terraform.tfstate"
#    - region: "us-east-1"
#    - encrypt: true
#    - dynamodb_table: "terraform-locks"
# 2. Adds a terraform_remote_state data source reading from:
#    the same S3 bucket but key: "production/network/terraform.tfstate"
# 3. Outputs the vpc_id from the remote state

terraform {
  # Add S3 backend here
}

# Remote state data source

# Output
hints: Backend config goes inside terraform {} block with 'backend "s3" {}'
DynamoDB table for locking uses dynamodb_table = "table-name"
Always enable encrypt = true for security compliance

validationRules.errorMessage: Add an S3 backend block inside terraform {}
Add DynamoDB table for state locking
Reference the remote state outputs in your output block

simulatedOutput.success:
Initializing the backend...
Successfully configured the backend "s3"!

Terraform will store state in: s3://my-company-terraform-state/production/app/terraform.tfstate
State locking: enabled (DynamoDB table: terraform-locks)
Encryption: enabled

Remote state data source: s3://my-company-terraform-state/production/network/terraform.tfstate
simulatedOutput.failure: Backend configuration error. Check S3 backend syntax, encryption flag, DynamoDB table, and remote state data source.
